revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841028]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 25|qq=November 25}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11th Month (Old Calendar)|1=November (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|Old Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|Rokuyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|Taian|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Events == * [[初始]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:9|9]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – In the 1st year of [[:en:Wang Mang|Wang Mang]], he founded the Xin dynasty, leading to the fall of the Former Han. * [[:en:養和|養和]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1182|1182]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]) – Empress Heitokuko of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]] was granted an honorary title, becoming known as Kenreimonin, as decreed in the "Meigetsuki" on January 1. * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|Kenkyū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1192|1192]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]) – Military commander [[Kumagai Naomitsu]] became a monk, joined [[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]], and took the name Renshō. * [[:en:Kōji (Muromachi period)|Kōji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1557|1557]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – [[:en:Mōri Motonari|Mōri Motonari]] recorded 14 precepts for his sons [[:en:Mōri Takamoto|Mōri Takamoto]], [[Yoshikawa Motoharu]], and [[:en:Kobayakawa Takakage|Kobayakawa Takakage]], later remembered as the story of "Three Arrows." * {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|Jōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1687|1687]] [[:en:December 29|December 29]]) – [[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]] departed on his journey as described in "Oku no Komon," famously beginning with "I shall be known as the traveler."
== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1009|1009]] [[:en:December 14|December 14]]) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. [[:en:1045|1045]]). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1594|1594]] [[:en:January 6|January 6]]) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. [[:en:1658|1658]]). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1820|1820]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. [[:en:1871|1871]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. [[:en:1933|1933]]). * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:December 25|December 25]]) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. [[:en:1944|1944]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1576|1576]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born [[:en:1528|1528]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1643|1643]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born [[:en:1567|1567]] or [[:en:1569|1569]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1755|1755]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]]) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born [[:en:1687|1687]]).
== Commemorations ==
== References ==
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|24|11|26|10|25|12|11|1125|qq=}}

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11th Month (Old Calendar)|1=November (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|Old Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|Rokuyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|Taian|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Events == * [[初始]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:9|9]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – In the 1st year of [[:en:Wang Mang|Wang Mang]], he founded the Xin dynasty, leading to the fall of the Former Han. * [[:en:養和|養和]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1182|1182]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]) – Empress Heitokuko of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]] was granted an honorary title, becoming known as Kenreimonin, as decreed in the "Meigetsuki" on January 1. * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|Kenkyū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1192|1192]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]) – Military commander [[Kumagai Naomitsu]] became a monk, joined [[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]], and took the name Renshō. * [[:en:Kōji (Muromachi period)|Kōji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1557|1557]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – [[:en:Mōri Motonari|Mōri Motonari]] recorded 14 precepts for his sons [[:en:Mōri Takamoto|Mōri Takamoto]], [[Yoshikawa Motoharu]], and [[:en:Kobayakawa Takakage|Kobayakawa Takakage]], later remembered as the story of "Three Arrows." * {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|Jōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1687|1687]] [[:en:December 29|December 29]]) – [[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]] departed on his journey as described in "Oku no Komon," famously beginning with "I shall be known as the traveler."
== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1009|1009]] [[:en:December 14|December 14]]) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. [[:en:1045|1045]]). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1594|1594]] [[:en:January 6|January 6]]) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. [[:en:1658|1658]]). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1820|1820]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. [[:en:1871|1871]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. [[:en:1933|1933]]). * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:December 25|December 25]]) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. [[:en:1944|1944]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1576|1576]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born [[:en:1528|1528]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1643|1643]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born [[:en:1567|1567]] or [[:en:1569|1569]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1755|1755]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]]) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born [[:en:1687|1687]]).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841028]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 25|qq=November 25}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11th Month (Old Calendar)|1=November (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|Old Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|Rokuyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|Taian|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Events == * [[初始]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:9|9]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – In the 1st year of [[:en:Wang Mang|Wang Mang]], he founded the Xin dynasty, leading to the fall of the Former Han. * [[:en:養和|養和]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1182|1182]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]) – Empress Heitokuko of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]] was granted an honorary title, becoming known as Kenreimonin, as decreed in the "Meigetsuki" on January 1. * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|Kenkyū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1192|1192]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]) – Military commander [[Kumagai Naomitsu]] became a monk, joined [[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]], and took the name Renshō. * [[:en:Kōji (Muromachi period)|Kōji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1557|1557]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – [[:en:Mōri Motonari|Mōri Motonari]] recorded 14 precepts for his sons [[:en:Mōri Takamoto|Mōri Takamoto]], [[Yoshikawa Motoharu]], and [[:en:Kobayakawa Takakage|Kobayakawa Takakage]], later remembered as the story of "Three Arrows." * {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|Jōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1687|1687]] [[:en:December 29|December 29]]) – [[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]] departed on his journey as described in "Oku no Komon," famously beginning with "I shall be known as the traveler."
== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1009|1009]] [[:en:December 14|December 14]]) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. [[:en:1045|1045]]). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1594|1594]] [[:en:January 6|January 6]]) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. [[:en:1658|1658]]). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1820|1820]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. [[:en:1871|1871]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. [[:en:1933|1933]]). * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:December 25|December 25]]) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. [[:en:1944|1944]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1576|1576]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born [[:en:1528|1528]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1643|1643]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born [[:en:1567|1567]] or [[:en:1569|1569]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1755|1755]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]]) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born [[:en:1687|1687]]).
== Commemorations ==
== References ==
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|24|11|26|10|25|12|11|1125|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841028]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 25|qq=November 25}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11th Month (Old Calendar)|1=November (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|Old Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|Rokuyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|Taian|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Events == * [[初始]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:9|9]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – In the 1st year of [[:en:Wang Mang|Wang Mang]], he founded the Xin dynasty, leading to the fall of the Former Han. * [[:en:養和|養和]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1182|1182]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]) – Empress Heitokuko of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]] was granted an honorary title, becoming known as Kenreimonin, as decreed in the "Meigetsuki" on January 1. * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|Kenkyū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1192|1192]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]) – Military commander [[Kumagai Naomitsu]] became a monk, joined [[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]], and took the name Renshō. * [[:en:Kōji (Muromachi period)|Kōji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1557|1557]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – [[:en:Mōri Motonari|Mōri Motonari]] recorded 14 precepts for his sons [[:en:Mōri Takamoto|Mōri Takamoto]], [[Yoshikawa Motoharu]], and [[:en:Kobayakawa Takakage|Kobayakawa Takakage]], later remembered as the story of "Three Arrows." * {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|Jōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1687|1687]] [[:en:December 29|December 29]]) – [[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]] departed on his journey as described in "Oku no Komon," famously beginning with "I shall be known as the traveler."
== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1009|1009]] [[:en:December 14|December 14]]) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. [[:en:1045|1045]]). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1594|1594]] [[:en:January 6|January 6]]) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. [[:en:1658|1658]]). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1820|1820]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. [[:en:1871|1871]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. [[:en:1933|1933]]). * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:December 25|December 25]]) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. [[:en:1944|1944]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1576|1576]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born [[:en:1528|1528]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1643|1643]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born [[:en:1567|1567]] or [[:en:1569|1569]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1755|1755]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]]) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born [[:en:1687|1687]]).
== Commemorations ==

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh_min_nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841028]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 25|qq=November 25}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11th Month (Old Calendar)|1=November (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|Old Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|Rokuyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|Taian|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Events == * [[初始]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:9|9]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – In the 1st year of [[:en:Wang Mang|Wang Mang]], he founded the Xin dynasty, leading to the fall of the Former Han. * [[:en:養和|養和]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1182|1182]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]) – Empress Heitokuko of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]] was granted an honorary title, becoming known as Kenreimonin, as decreed in the "Meigetsuki" on January 1. * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|Kenkyū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1192|1192]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]) – Military commander [[Kumagai Naomitsu]] became a monk, joined [[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]], and took the name Renshō. * [[:en:Kōji (Muromachi period)|Kōji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1557|1557]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – [[:en:Mōri Motonari|Mōri Motonari]] recorded 14 precepts for his sons [[:en:Mōri Takamoto|Mōri Takamoto]], [[Yoshikawa Motoharu]], and [[:en:Kobayakawa Takakage|Kobayakawa Takakage]], later remembered as the story of "Three Arrows." * {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|Jōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1687|1687]] [[:en:December 29|December 29]]) – [[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]] departed on his journey as described in "Oku no Komon," famously beginning with "I shall be known as the traveler."
== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1009|1009]] [[:en:December 14|December 14]]) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. [[:en:1045|1045]]). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1594|1594]] [[:en:January 6|January 6]]) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. [[:en:1658|1658]]). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1820|1820]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. [[:en:1871|1871]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. [[:en:1933|1933]]). * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:December 25|December 25]]) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. [[:en:1944|1944]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1576|1576]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born [[:en:1528|1528]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1643|1643]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born [[:en:1567|1567]] or [[:en:1569|1569]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1755|1755]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]]) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born [[:en:1687|1687]]).
== Commemorations ==
== References ==

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh_min_nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh_min_nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841028]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 25|qq=November 25}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11th Month (Old Calendar)|1=November (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|Old Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|Rokuyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|Taian|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Events == * [[初始]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:9|9]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – In the 1st year of [[:en:Wang Mang|Wang Mang]], he founded the Xin dynasty, leading to the fall of the Former Han. * [[:en:養和|養和]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1182|1182]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]) – Empress Heitokuko of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]] was granted an honorary title, becoming known as Kenreimonin, as decreed in the "Meigetsuki" on January 1. * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|Kenkyū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1192|1192]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]) – Military commander [[Kumagai Naomitsu]] became a monk, joined [[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]], and took the name Renshō. * [[:en:Kōji (Muromachi period)|Kōji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1557|1557]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – [[:en:Mōri Motonari|Mōri Motonari]] recorded 14 precepts for his sons [[:en:Mōri Takamoto|Mōri Takamoto]], [[Yoshikawa Motoharu]], and [[:en:Kobayakawa Takakage|Kobayakawa Takakage]], later remembered as the story of "Three Arrows." * {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|Jōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1687|1687]] [[:en:December 29|December 29]]) – [[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]] departed on his journey as described in "Oku no Komon," famously beginning with "I shall be known as the traveler."
== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1009|1009]] [[:en:December 14|December 14]]) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. [[:en:1045|1045]]). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1594|1594]] [[:en:January 6|January 6]]) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. [[:en:1658|1658]]). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1820|1820]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. [[:en:1871|1871]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. [[:en:1933|1933]]). * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:December 25|December 25]]) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. [[:en:1944|1944]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1576|1576]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born [[:en:1528|1528]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1643|1643]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born [[:en:1567|1567]] or [[:en:1569|1569]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1755|1755]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]]) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born [[:en:1687|1687]]).
== Commemorations ==
== References ==

[[:en:1933|1933]]). * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:December 25|December 25]]) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. [[:en:1944|1944]]).

revision.comment: Text replacement - "zh_min_nan" to "zh-min-nan"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841028]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 25|qq=November 25}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11th Month (Old Calendar)|1=November (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|Old Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|Rokuyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|Taian|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Events == * [[初始]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:9|9]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – In the 1st year of [[:en:Wang Mang|Wang Mang]], he founded the Xin dynasty, leading to the fall of the Former Han. * [[:en:養和|養和]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1182|1182]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]) – Empress Heitokuko of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]] was granted an honorary title, becoming known as Kenreimonin, as decreed in the "Meigetsuki" on January 1. * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|Kenkyū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1192|1192]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]) – Military commander [[Kumagai Naomitsu]] became a monk, joined [[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]], and took the name Renshō. * [[:en:Kōji (Muromachi period)|Kōji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1557|1557]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – [[:en:Mōri Motonari|Mōri Motonari]] recorded 14 precepts for his sons [[:en:Mōri Takamoto|Mōri Takamoto]], [[Yoshikawa Motoharu]], and [[:en:Kobayakawa Takakage|Kobayakawa Takakage]], later remembered as the story of "Three Arrows." * {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|Jōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1687|1687]] [[:en:December 29|December 29]]) – [[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]] departed on his journey as described in "Oku no Komon," famously beginning with "I shall be known as the traveler."
== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1009|1009]] [[:en:December 14|December 14]]) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. [[:en:1045|1045]]). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1594|1594]] [[:en:January 6|January 6]]) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. [[:en:1658|1658]]). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1820|1820]] [[:en:January 10|January 10]]) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. [[:en:1871|1871]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. [[:en:1933|1933]]). * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:December 25|December 25]]) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. [[:en:1944|1944]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1576|1576]] [[:en:December 15|December 15]]) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born [[:en:1528|1528]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1643|1643]] [[:en:January 15|January 15]]) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born [[:en:1567|1567]] or [[:en:1569|1569]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1755|1755]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]]) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born [[:en:1687|1687]]).

== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1009|en|1009|WD=Q19236|lt=1009}} {{ill|December 14|en|December 14|WD=Q2382|lt=December 14}}) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. {{ill|1045|en|1045|WD=Q19351|lt=1045}}). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1594|en|1594|WD=Q6777|lt=1594}} {{ill|January 6|en|January 6|WD=Q2221|lt=January 6}}) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. {{ill|1658|en|1658|WD=Q6991|lt=1658}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1820|en|1820|WD=Q6973|lt=1820}} {{ill|January 10|en|January 10|WD=Q2243|lt=January 10}}) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}}). * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15}}) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. {{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933}}). * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25}}) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1576|en|1576|WD=Q6700|lt=1576}} {{ill|December 15|en|December 15|WD=Q2383|lt=December 15}}) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born {{ill|1528|en|1528|WD=Q6415|lt=1528}}). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643}} {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15}}) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born {{ill|1567|en|1567|WD=Q6642|lt=1567}} or {{ill|1569|en|1569|WD=Q6653|lt=1569}}). * {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7}}) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born {{ill|1687|en|1687|WD=Q7668|lt=1687}}).

{{Not to be confused with|November 25|qq=November 25}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11th Month (Old Calendar)|1=November (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|Old Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|Rokuyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|Taian|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Events == * [[初始]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|9|en|9|WD=Q19108|lt=9}} {{ill|January 10|en|January 10|WD=Q2243|lt=January 10}}) – In the 1st year of {{ill|Wang Mang|en|Wang Mang|WD=Q7250|lt=Wang Mang}}, he founded the Xin dynasty, leading to the fall of the Former Han. * [[:en:養和|養和]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1182|en|1182|WD=Q19708|lt=1182}} {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}) – Empress Heitokuko of {{ill|Emperor Takakura|en|Emperor Takakura|WD=Q357261|lt=Emperor Takakura}} was granted an honorary title, becoming known as Kenreimonin, as decreed in the "Meigetsuki" on January 1. * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|Kenkyū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1192|en|1192|WD=Q19739|lt=1192}} {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31}}) – Military commander [[Kumagai Naomitsu]] became a monk, joined {{ill|Hōnen|en|Hōnen|WD=Q507980|lt=Hōnen}}, and took the name Renshō. * {{ill|Kōji (Muromachi period)|en|Kōji (Muromachi period)|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1557|en|1557|WD=Q6595|lt=1557}} {{ill|December 15|en|December 15|WD=Q2383|lt=December 15}}) – {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|WD=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari}} recorded 14 precepts for his sons {{ill|Mōri Takamoto|en|Mōri Takamoto|WD=Q859795|lt=Mōri Takamoto}}, [[Yoshikawa Motoharu]], and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|en|Kobayakawa Takakage|WD=Q1143508|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage}}, later remembered as the story of "Three Arrows." * {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|Jōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1687|en|1687|WD=Q7668|lt=1687}} {{ill|December 29|en|December 29|WD=Q2862|lt=December 29}}) – {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō}} departed on his journey as described in "Oku no Komon," famously beginning with "I shall be known as the traveler."
== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1009|en|1009|WD=Q19236|lt=1009}} {{ill|December 14|en|December 14|WD=Q2382|lt=December 14}}) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. {{ill|1045|en|1045|WD=Q19351|lt=1045}}). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1594|en|1594|WD=Q6777|lt=1594}} {{ill|January 6|en|January 6|WD=Q2221|lt=January 6}}) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. {{ill|1658|en|1658|WD=Q6991|lt=1658}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1820|en|1820|WD=Q6973|lt=1820}} {{ill|January 10|en|January 10|WD=Q2243|lt=January 10}}) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}}). * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15}}) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. {{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933}}). * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25}}) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1576|en|1576|WD=Q6700|lt=1576}} {{ill|December 15|en|December 15|WD=Q2383|lt=December 15}}) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born {{ill|1528|en|1528|WD=Q6415|lt=1528}}). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643}} {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15}}) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born {{ill|1567|en|1567|WD=Q6642|lt=1567}} or {{ill|1569|en|1569|WD=Q6653|lt=1569}}). * {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7}}) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born {{ill|1687|en|1687|WD=Q7668|lt=1687}}).

calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} {{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25|1=December 25}}) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1576|en|1576|WD=Q6700|lt=1576|1=1576}} {{ill|December 15|en|December 15|WD=Q2383|lt=December 15|1=December 15}}) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born {{ill|1528|en|1528|WD=Q6415|lt=1528|1=1528}}). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643|1=1643}} {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15|1=January 15}}) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born {{ill|1567|en|1567|WD=Q6642|lt=1567|1=1567}} or {{ill|1569|en|1569|WD=Q6653|lt=1569|1=1569}}). * {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755|1=1755}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}}) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born {{ill|1687|en|1687|WD=Q7668|lt=1687|1=1687}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿五]][[zh:十一月廿五]]
{{Not to be confused with|November 25|qq=November 25}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11th Month (Old Calendar)|1=November (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|Old Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|Rokuyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|Taian|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Events == * [[初始]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|9|en|9|WD=Q19108|lt=9|1=9}} {{ill|January 10|en|January 10|WD=Q2243|lt=January 10|1=January 10}}) – In the 1st year of {{ill|Wang Mang|en|Wang Mang|WD=Q7250|lt=Wang Mang|1=Wang Mang}}, he founded the Xin dynasty, leading to the fall of the Former Han. * [[:en:養和|養和]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1182|en|1182|WD=Q19708|lt=1182|1=1182}} {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}) – Empress Heitokuko of {{ill|Emperor Takakura|en|Emperor Takakura|WD=Q357261|lt=Emperor Takakura|1=Emperor Takakura}} was granted an honorary title, becoming known as Kenreimonin, as decreed in the "Meigetsuki" on January 1. * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|Kenkyū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1192|en|1192|WD=Q19739|lt=1192|1=1192}} {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31|1=December 31}}) – Military commander [[Kumagai Naomitsu]] became a monk, joined {{ill|Hōnen|en|Hōnen|WD=Q507980|lt=Hōnen|1=Hōnen}}, and took the name Renshō. * {{ill|Kōji (Muromachi period)|en|Kōji (Muromachi period)|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1557|en|1557|WD=Q6595|lt=1557|1=1557}} {{ill|December 15|en|December 15|WD=Q2383|lt=December 15|1=December 15}}) – {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|WD=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari}} recorded 14 precepts for his sons {{ill|Mōri Takamoto|en|Mōri Takamoto|WD=Q859795|lt=Mōri Takamoto|1=Mōri Takamoto}}, [[Yoshikawa Motoharu]], and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|en|Kobayakawa Takakage|WD=Q1143508|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage|1=Kobayakawa Takakage}}, later remembered as the story of "Three Arrows." * {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|Jōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1687|en|1687|WD=Q7668|lt=1687|1=1687}} {{ill|December 29|en|December 29|WD=Q2862|lt=December 29|1=December 29}}) – {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}} departed on his journey as described in "Oku no Komon," famously beginning with "I shall be known as the traveler."
== Births == * {{ill|Kankō|ja|Kankō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 6th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1009|en|1009|WD=Q19236|lt=1009|1=1009}} {{ill|December 14|en|December 14|WD=Q2382|lt=December 14|1=December 14}}) – Emperor Go-Suzaku, the 69th Emperor of Japan (d. {{ill|1045|en|1045|WD=Q19351|lt=1045|1=1045}}). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1594|en|1594|WD=Q6777|lt=1594|1=1594}} {{ill|January 6|en|January 6|WD=Q2221|lt=January 6|1=January 6}}) – Maeda Toshitsune, the 3rd lord of the Kaga Domain (d. {{ill|1658|en|1658|WD=Q6991|lt=1658|1=1658}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|Bunsei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1820|en|1820|WD=Q6973|lt=1820|1=1820}} {{ill|January 10|en|January 10|WD=Q2243|lt=January 10|1=January 10}}) – Ando Nobumasa, a Rōjū and the 5th lord of Iwaki-Heiban in Mutsu Province (d. {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}}). * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15|1=January 15}}) – Sakai Toshihiko, a socialist activist (d. {{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}). * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} {{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25|1=December 25}}) – Soeda Asenbō, an enka singer (d. {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenshō|ja|Tenshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1576|en|1576|WD=Q6700|lt=1576|1=1576}} {{ill|December 15|en|December 15|WD=Q2383|lt=December 15|1=December 15}}) – Kitabatake Tomonori, the 8th head of the Kitabatake family and governor of Ise (born {{ill|1528|en|1528|WD=Q6415|lt=1528|1=1528}}). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|Kan'ei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643|1=1643}} {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15|1=January 15}}) – Tachibana Muneshige, lord of the Tanakura Domain and the Yanagawa Domain (born {{ill|1567|en|1567|WD=Q6642|lt=1567|1=1567}} or {{ill|1569|en|1569|WD=Q6653|lt=1569|1=1569}}). * {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755|1=1755}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}}) – Nabeshima Muneshige, lord of the Saga Domain (born {{ill|1687|en|1687|WD=Q7668|lt=1687|1=1687}}).
